repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_angle0_rejected: functional-null bridge phase geometry already used the same null-annihilator/sharp-interval idea, but not the regularizer-selection kernel

published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023SPL and BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023COLT: functionals may be identified when bridge/nuisance functions are nonunique
DarollesFanFlorensRenault2011Econometrica and BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023COLT: Tikhonov/penalized nuisance limits are known; finite exact-fiber causal selector framing is new

repo_prior_art.cite: API §3 ExactID / PartialID seed catalogue plus cited pid_mediation_proxy and eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator artifacts do not contain proximal ATE quadratic-selector selection ranges

published_prior_art.cite: Closest verified papers characterize point identification, strong functional identification, partial proxy bounds, or penalized estimation limits, but not open-class SPD selector coverage of the sharp proximal ATE interval
MiaoGengTchetgen2018Biometrika and ShiMiaoNelsonTchetgen2020JRSSB use finite/categorical proxy structures for point identification, not a nondegenerate binary penalty-dependence witness

novelty_flags.label: global literature checklist
novelty_flags.one_line: Drafter handoff says the named literature checklist is missing; §5 is usable for reconstruction but the required 4-10 paper checklist was not emitted.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the kernel is a new regularizer-selection range, but it does not yet satisfy the flagship rubric via a named estimator equivalence/non-equivalence frontier, a cited open strict-extension class, or a new sharp/tighter identification bound.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add the missing literature checklist with author, year, venue, bibkey, one-line claim, and the §8 result each paper tests; include Zhang et al. 2023 and Bennett et al. 2023 as load-bearing comparator papers, not just background.

To meet the flagship floor, recast Conjecture 1 as an iff frontier between at least two named proximal bridge workflows or estimators, with a computable observable threshold separating penalty-invariant and penalty-dependent regimes.
Sharpen the Cui and Kallus workflow comparisons by naming the exact bridge subproblem and clarifying whether the statement covers outcome bridges only or also treatment-bridge/IPW components.
For Conjecture 2, state the binary witness as explicit finite matrices for P(W|Z,A), m(Z,A), and the ATE loading, so the nondegeneracy and one-dimensional fiber claims become directly auditable.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal to supply the missing checklist and either lift Conjecture 1 to a flagship-qualified estimator-frontier/open-regime claim or lower the novelty target to field.
